en:
to bounce back

Example:
He bounced back quickly.

to downplay

Example:
He tried to downplay the problem.

to minimize

Example:
Don’t minimize my feelings.

to judge

Example:
Please don’t judge me.

to condemn

Example:
They condemned his behavior.

to devalue

Example:
Don’t devalue me.

to undervalue

Example:
He constantly undervalues my efforts.

to diminish

Example:
Don’t diminish me.

to invalidate

Example:
Don’t invalidate my feelings.
attraction — repulsion
contraction — expansion

almost done

Example:
Dinner is almost done.

almost finished

Example:
I’m almost finished.

nearly there

Example:
We’re nearly there.

to get closure

Example:
I need to get closure.

to find closure

Example:
She finally found closure.

to finish something unresolved

Example:
I want to finish something unresolved.

to put something behind me

Example:
I just want to put it behind me.

to wrap things up

Example:
Let’s wrap things up.
safety / security

to be safe

Example:
I want to be safe.

to feel safe

Example:
I feel safe here.

to worry too much about one’s safety

Example:
She worries too much about her safety.

to be overly concerned about one’s safety

Example:
He is overly concerned about his safety.

to be too focused on staying safe

Example:
I’m too focused on staying safe.

distinctly

Example:
I distinctly remember closing the door.

ru: после того, как прошёл или перенёс что-то
en:
after undergoing

Example:
After undergoing surgery.

diminutive

Example:
"Kitty" is a diminutive of "cat".

diminutive form

Example:
This is a diminutive form of a name.

affectionate diminutive

Example:
"Sweetie" is an affectionate diminutive.
